questions.text: The Strait of Malacca separates which two landmasses?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Malacca separates the Malay Peninsula (Malaysia/Thailand) from the Indonesian island of Sumatra. It is the world's busiest shipping strait, connecting the Indian Ocean to the South China Sea/Pacific, carrying ~25% of global trade.

questions.text: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean (~165 million km²) is the largest ocean, covering more area than all of Earth's land combined. It contains the deepest point (Mariana Trench) and the Ring of Fire.

questions.text: The Gulf Stream is a major ocean current that flows through the:
questions.explanation: The Gulf Stream is a powerful warm ocean current in the North Atlantic Ocean. It originates in the Gulf of Mexico, flows up the eastern coast of North America, and crosses the Atlantic toward Europe as the North Atlantic Drift, moderating Western Europe's climate.

questions.text: The Palk Strait lies between which two countries?
questions.explanation: The Palk Strait separates India (Tamil Nadu) from Sri Lanka. It connects the Bay of Bengal (north) with the Gulf of Mannar (south). Adam's Bridge (Rama Setu), a chain of limestone shoals, lies across this shallow strait.

questions.text: The Dead Sea is the saltiest large body of water AND:
options: The largest lake in the world
The deepest lake in the world
questions.explanation: The Dead Sea shoreline (Jordan/Israel/West Bank) is the lowest point on land at ~430 m below sea level. The Dead Sea is also extremely saline (~340 g/L vs ocean's ~35 g/L). Note: Don Juan Pond in Antarctica is saltier but much smaller.

questions.text: El Niño typically causes which effect on Indian monsoon?
options: Has no effect on Indian monsoon
Causes the monsoon to arrive early
questions.explanation: El Niño (warming of central-eastern Pacific) usually weakens the Indian SW monsoon, creating drought tendencies. La Niña (cooling of Pacific) usually strengthens the monsoon. However, the relationship is not absolute — some El Niño years have had normal monsoons.

questions.text: The Suez Canal connects which two bodies of water?
questions.explanation: The Suez Canal (Egypt, 193 km) connects the Mediterranean Sea (north) to the Red Sea (south), providing a shortcut between Europe and Asia without going around Africa. Unlike the Panama Canal, the Suez Canal has NO locks — it is at sea level.

questions.text: Which ocean current keeps the climate of Western Europe mild?
questions.explanation: The Gulf Stream (warm current from Gulf of Mexico) and its extension, the North Atlantic Drift, carry warm tropical water toward NW Europe. Without this, UK, Norway, and other Atlantic-facing countries would be 5–10°C colder, similar to Newfoundland at the same latitude.

questions.text: Approximately what percentage of world's petroleum passes through the Strait of Hormuz?
questions.explanation: Approximately 20% of the world's petroleum (and 30-35% of globally traded oil) passes through the Strait of Hormuz between Iran and Oman. It is the world's most important oil chokepoint, connecting the Persian Gulf to the Arabian Sea.

questions.text: Which ocean was officially recognized as the 5th ocean by the International Hydrographic Organization (IHO) in 2000?
questions.explanation: The Southern Ocean (surrounding Antarctica) was officially recognized as the 5th ocean by the IHO in 2000. It is defined by the Antarctic Circumpolar Current and is the 4th largest ocean by area (~21 million km²).

questions.text: Cold ocean currents on the western coasts of continents tend to create:
options: Desert conditions due to dry, stable air
questions.explanation: Cold ocean currents (like Benguela off SW Africa, Humboldt off South America, California Current off W USA) create cool, stable atmospheric conditions that suppress rainfall, contributing to desert formation on adjacent coastlines. This explains the Namib Desert, Atacama Desert, and Baja California dryness.

questions.text: The Bab-el-Mandeb Strait is strategically important because:
options: It connects the Pacific and Indian Oceans
It controls access to the Suez Canal from the south
It is the world's busiest shipping route
It separates India from Sri Lanka
questions.explanation: Bab-el-Mandeb (between Yemen and Djibouti) connects the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden/Indian Ocean. Ships using the Suez Canal must pass through Bab-el-Mandeb at the Red Sea's southern end. Its name means 'Gate of Tears' in Arabic.

questions.text: The Indian Ocean is unique among oceans because:
options: It is entirely in the Eastern Hemisphere and is the only ocean named after a country
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean is the only ocean named after a country (India). It is also entirely in the Eastern Hemisphere, bounded by Asia, Africa, and Australia. It is the 3rd largest ocean and the warmest ocean.

questions.text: Which ocean current causes the Namib Desert to be dry along the coast of Southwest Africa?
questions.explanation: The Benguela Current (cold) flows northward along the coast of South Africa and Namibia. Cold water cools and stabilizes the air above it, suppressing rainfall along the coast and creating the Namib Desert. It also creates rich fishing grounds due to upwelling.

questions.text: The Panama Canal connects which two oceans?
questions.explanation: The Panama Canal (80 km, opened 1914) connects the Atlantic Ocean (via Caribbean Sea) to the Pacific Ocean through the Isthmus of Panama. Unlike the Suez Canal, it uses locks to lift ships up to Gatun Lake and then lower them down.

questions.text: The Caspian Sea is correctly described as:
options: An arm of the Pacific Ocean
The world's largest saltwater lake/landlocked water body
A freshwater lake in Central Asia
An inland sea connected to the Indian Ocean
questions.explanation: The Caspian Sea is the world's largest landlocked water body by area (~371,000 km²). It is saline (though less salty than ocean water) and is classified as both a lake and a sea by different authorities. It borders Russia, Azerbaijan, Iran, Turkmenistan, and Kazakhstan.
